content:
If we stick strictly to physical disabilities

Doll - a brain in a vat operating what is effectively a full body prosthetic
Nymphaea - chronic illness
Alva - prosthetic leg
Pandia - will be extinguished by water
Karen - a zombie, probably with special diet and accommodation requirements
Lily - recovering from a bajillion surgical procedures
"24" - idk where to even start
Ryker - a dog, with diet and accommodation requirements

Celeste - a boat, and also chronically nonexistent
Caecus - a fish, with diet and accommodation requirements
Goliath - a fish
GMurphy - idk where to even start

Aigis - lost use of one eye
Leigh - blood-only diet
Faint - confined to her cradle so she doesnt kill everyone with radiation
Helot - multiple scars and long-term injuries, also melting
Uvhash - has a hole in his chest
GHelot - made of chains, probably really inconvenient in daily life
Doresain - a zombie

Erica - a robot, dependent on power supply, and also attached to a whole bunch of inconvenient machinery
Winkle - on life support, attached to her locator
Horla - lost use of one eye, also made of ink
Castor and Pollux - whatever is going on with the sacred heart (keter-class object of mass destruction in pollux's body)
nods nods, makes sense and all very understandable
theres also a lot of edge cases like agrippa where idk if razor sharp tippy toe heels count as a disability
i will again state that you cannot convince me that pollux doesn't have phantom pain from where his wings used to be. him and his brother absolutely have chronic pain
*mmhm*

So, I'm new here and I've been speedrunning the story up until chapter 7. And my question is - are there any prerequisite side-stories I should be reading before getting into chapter 7 story? Because the start of chapter 7, right after finishing chapter 6, felt like I've missed a lot of things in-between.
In general, can look in here https://docs.google.com/document/d/1K72xeUFXunaR8WqwEN1YyEPVyd09q71KJHoBKROhoho/edit?tab=t.0

embeds.title: Niko's Event Unlock Recommendations
embeds.description: Since the in-game side story list is not in chronological order, this is a spoiler-free list of side stories I recommend to those interested in the lore of Morimens to read and in what order.  This list is also a spoiler-free companion list to my (spoiler full) Morimens timeline project, where I ...

content: Can I ask which one specifically I should read to understand what even happened in between chapters 6 and 7? Is it the one with Gramona?
there's none that specifically covers the transition between 6 and 7, mostly because 6 and 7 are separate plot arcs so there's not really any transition to be had
but time wise you should read gramona before ch7 and probably salvatore as well to get some background on the things that'll happen in ch7
